sanskrit:
श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति।
शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति॥
transliteration:
śrotrādīnīndriyāṇy anye saṃyamāgniṣu juhvati,
śabdādīn viṣayān anya indriyāgniṣu juhvati.
literal_meaning: Some offer the senses such as hearing into the fire of restraint; others offer the sense-objects such as sound into the fire of the senses.
contextual_meaning: Two complementary self-regulation strategies: (1) withdraw the sense organ itself (internal shutdown) or (2) withdraw the tempting stimulus (external boundary). Both are legitimate yogic sacrifices that convert raw impulse into disciplined energy.
psychological_state: Approach-avoidance conflict resolved by choosing either stimulus removal or response suppression, both framed as heroic offerings rather than deprivation.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m. and realizing you can either delete the app (burn the object) or put the phone in another room (burn the organ).
anchor_text: Shut the door or walk away—both starve the fire of impulse.
